solutions.solution: c; I + I = No conclusion. But the choices make a complementary pair. One statement is always true.
d; I + E = O → Some parrots are not green. But this conclusion is not given among the choices. → All clerks are lazy. → A + I = No conclusion. → Some persons are green.
b; Some men are doctors.
d; A + I = No conclusion.
b; I + I = O

c; No conclusion can be derived as there are four terms instead of three. Two premises, flying beings, non-living beings and one of the two facts. All parrots are living beings and 'Some cats are parrots' – this premise is incorrect.
a; Choices make a pair. But this conclusion is not true.
c; Choices make a complementary pair.

d; I + I = No conclusion. But on second thought, any soldier is famous or intelligent or none of these. Hence, either of the conclusion A + E = O may be concluded. But the choices make a complementary pair.
a; All wheels are machines + Some machines are tractors = A + I = No conclusion.
d; Align : All wheels are machines + Some machines are tractors = A + I = No conclusion.
a; A + E = E. No roads are houses. But this conclusion is not given among the choices.
b; I + A = I
c; Align : Sohan is tall + Some tall are players = A + I = No conclusion. Sohan should either be a player or a non-player. Hence, other choice.
a; A + E = E
d; I + A = No conclusion.
b; All horses are dogs → on convention → Some dogs are horses.
a; All beds are towels → on convention → Some towels are beds.

c; Align: Some men are thieves + No boy is a thief = Some men are thieves + No thieves are boys (converted) = I + E = O → Some men are not boys. But this conclusion if follows from the first statement obviously.
c; A + E = E; ∴ No dresses are brown. Also, All dresses are shoes → on convention → Some shoes are dresses.
d; A + I + A = Some hens are horses. Again, Some hens are horses → a → conversion → Some horses are hens.
a; Some chairs are stools → on conversion → Some stools are chairs.
a; No conclusion can be derived from the two given statements. But only A is correct I must be true nevertheless because all dogs bite.
a; A + E = E
b; Some trees are horses → on conversion → Some horses are trees.
d; All cows are rambhas + Some rambhas are buffaloes = A + I = I conclusion.
c; I + A = I; ∴ Some moons are radios. Also, Some rooms are stoves → on conversion → ∴ Some stoves are rooms.
